tasks.description: Fix frontend .env.local to use the actual LiveKit Cloud URL instead of placeholder, so LiveKitRoom can connect.
steps: Update `ui-web/.env.local` — change `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEKIT_URL=wss://your-project.livekit.cloud` to `NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVEKIT_URL=wss://siestai-qk2c7bgk.livekit.cloud`

tasks.description: Add ArenaLiveState type to the shared types file. Reuse existing TokenResponse from livekit.ts for the API response (same shape). Also extend ArenaWsServerMessage system variant to include 'session_started' event.
steps: Add `ArenaLiveState` interface to `ui-web/src/lib/types.ts` with fields: `roomName: string`, `token: string`, `serverUrl: string`
Extend the `ArenaWsServerMessage` union's `system` type variant: change `event: 'participant_joined' | 'participant_left'` to `event: 'participant_joined' | 'participant_left' | 'session_started'`
Add optional `roomName?: string` to the system variant (only present for session_started)

tasks.description: Add RoomServiceClient to LivekitService and create generateArenaToken method that creates a LiveKit room with arena metadata and returns a participant JWT.
steps: Import `RoomServiceClient` from `livekit-server-sdk` in `backend/src/livekit/livekit.service.ts`

Add a `private roomService: RoomServiceClient` field, initialize it in constructor by converting LIVEKIT_URL from `wss://` to `https://` (pattern: `.replace('wss://', 'https://').replace('ws://', 'http://')`)

Add `generateArenaToken(session: ArenaSession)` method that: (1) generates room name `arena-{random-8-chars}`, (2) generates identity `user-{random-8-chars}`, (3) builds metadata JSON `{ type: 'arena', agents: session.participants.filter(p => p.type === 'native_agent').map(p => ({ name: p.name, instructions: p.instructions || '' })), mode: session.mode, topic: session.topic, participationMode: session.participationMode }`, (4) validates metadata < 60KB, (5) calls `this.roomService.createRoom({ name: roomName, metadata: metadataJson })`, (6) creates AccessToken with `canPublish: session.participationMode === 'human_collab'`, `canPublishData: true`, `canSubscribe: true`, and RoomConfiguration with agent dispatch
Import ArenaSession type from `../arena/arena.interfaces`
Export LivekitService from LivekitModule so ArenaModule can import it — add `exports: [LivekitService]` to `livekit.module.ts`

tasks.description: Add startSession method to ArenaService that transitions session to 'active' and stores the LiveKit room name.
steps: Add optional `roomName?: string` field to `ArenaSession` interface in `backend/src/arena/arena.interfaces.ts`
Add `startSession(sessionId: string, roomName: string)` method to ArenaService — validates session exists and status is 'waiting', sets status to 'active', stores roomName
Throw BadRequestException if session is not in 'waiting' status

tasks.description: Add POST /arena/sessions/:id/start endpoint that creates a LiveKit room and returns connection details to the host.
steps: Import LivekitModule into ArenaModule (`imports: [LivekitModule]`) so LivekitService is available for injection
Inject `LivekitService` into ArenaController constructor alongside existing ArenaService and InvitationService
Add `@Post('sessions/:id/start')` handler to `backend/src/arena/arena.controller.ts`

Handler: (1) calls `arenaService.getSession(id)` to validate session exists, (2) calls `livekitService.generateArenaToken(session)` to create room + token, (3) calls `arenaService.startSession(id, result.roomName)` to mark session as active, (4) returns `{ token: result.token, serverUrl: result.serverUrl, roomName: result.roomName }`

tasks.description: Add fetchArenaToken function to frontend LiveKit utilities that calls the start endpoint.
steps: Add `fetchArenaToken(sessionId: string): Promise<TokenResponse>` to `ui-web/src/lib/livekit.ts`
Calls `POST ${API_URL}/arena/sessions/${sessionId}/start`
Reuse existing `TokenResponse` type (already has token, serverUrl, roomName)

tasks.description: Extend ArenaSessionContext to manage LiveKit connection state (token, serverUrl, roomName) alongside the existing WebSocket state.
steps: Import `ArenaLiveState` from types and `fetchArenaToken` from livekit
Add `liveState: ArenaLiveState | null` state to the provider
Add `liveState` to the context value interface
Add `startCall: () => Promise<void>` method: checks `session` is non-null, calls `fetchArenaToken(session.id)`, stores `{ roomName, token, serverUrl }` in `liveState`
Add `startCall` to the context value
Keep existing WebSocket connection alive when startCall is invoked — it's used for invitation coordination and external agent text relay
Clear `liveState` to null in `endSession`
Handle `session_started` event in WS onmessage switch — just log for now, add to existing `system` case

tasks.description: Create ArenaRoom component that renders participant tiles inside a LiveKitRoom context with audio playback, active speaker detection, and call controls.
steps: Create `ui-web/src/components/arena/arena-room.tsx`
Props: `participants: ArenaParticipant[]`, `participationMode: ParticipationMode`, `topic?: string`, `onEndSession: () => void`
Use `useVoiceAssistant()` hook to get agent state and transcripts (same pattern as live page `ActiveSession` component at `ui-web/src/app/live/page.tsx:78`)
Use `useConversationTranscript()` hook from `ui-web/src/hooks/use-conversation-transcript.ts` for transcript messages
Parse agent transcript messages for `[AgentName]: text` prefix to detect active speaker — extract speaker name via regex `/^\[([^\]]+)\]:/`
Render native agent tiles as a responsive grid — highlight the active speaker's tile with speaking ring animation (copy ring CSS pattern from ExternalParticipantTile)
Render a 'You' tile when participationMode is 'human_collab' showing mic status via `useLocalParticipant()`
Include `<RoomAudioRenderer />` from `@livekit/components-react` for audio playback
Include `CallTimer` from `@/components/live/call-timer` for duration display
Include 'End Session' button calling `onEndSession`

tasks.description: Create a transcript sidebar for the arena that shows messages attributed to each agent persona with colored names.
steps: Create `ui-web/src/components/arena/arena-transcript.tsx`
Props: `participants: ArenaParticipant[]` (for name→color mapping), `messages: TranscriptMessage[]`, `isOpen: boolean`, `onToggle: () => void`
Parse `[AgentName]: text` format from each message.text — extract speaker name, look up color from participants array by name match
Display each message with a colored dot and agent name header, then the message text
User messages (sender === 'user') show as 'You' with cyan accent
Collapsible sidebar toggled by `isOpen` prop (same layout pattern as `ui-web/src/components/live/transcript-sidebar.tsx`)
Auto-scroll to bottom on new messages using a ref and useEffect

tasks.description: Wire the arena page's 'live' state to mount LiveKitRoom with the arena token and render ArenaRoom inside it.
steps: In `ui-web/src/app/arena/page.tsx`, update `handleStartCall` to: `setPageState('live')` then call `await arenaSession.startCall()` — wrap in try/catch, revert to 'waiting' on failure
Replace the static 'live' state JSX (lines 533-602) with a conditional: if `arenaSession.liveState` is null, show a connecting spinner; if non-null, render `<LiveKitRoom>` wrapper
LiveKitRoom props: `serverUrl={arenaSession.liveState.serverUrl}`, `token={arenaSession.liveState.token}`, `...ROOM_OPTIONS`, `connect={true}`
Inside LiveKitRoom, render `<ArenaRoom participants={arenaSession.participants} participationMode={participationMode} topic={topic} onEndSession={handleEndSession} />`
Import `LiveKitRoom` from `@livekit/components-react`, `ROOM_OPTIONS` from `@/lib/livekit`, `ArenaRoom` from `@/components/arena/arena-room`
Import `@livekit/components-styles` at top of file
Keep 'End Session' functionality — calls `handleEndSession` which already calls `arenaSession.endSession()` and sets pageState to 'ended'

tasks.description: Extend the siestai-agent to read arena room metadata and orchestrate multi-persona conversations with speaker attribution.
steps: Create `agent/src/arena-agent.ts` extending `voice.Agent`
Constructor accepts `agents: { name: string, instructions: string }[]`, `mode: string`, `topic?: string`, `participationMode: string`

Build system instructions that: (1) list all persona names and their instructions, (2) require the LLM to prefix every response with `[PersonaName]: ` for speaker attribution, (3) for 'group' mode + human_collab: instruct personas to respond naturally to user input, switching between personas based on relevance, (4) for 'agent_only': instruct personas to simulate a multi-turn discussion on the topic, alternating speakers
In `agent/src/main.ts`, after `ctx.connect()` and before `session.start()`, read `ctx.room.metadata` — if it exists, parse as JSON
If `metadata.type === 'arena'`, construct `new ArenaAgent(metadata.agents, metadata.mode, metadata.topic, metadata.participationMode)` instead of `new Agent()`
For agent_only mode, use `session.generateReply()` with instructions to begin the discussion on the topic instead of greeting the user

tasks.description: Add broadcastSessionStarted method to ArenaGateway and call it when the host starts the call, so WebSocket-connected clients know the session is live.
steps: Add a public `broadcastSessionStarted(sessionId: string, roomName: string)` method to ArenaGateway that broadcasts `{ type: 'system', event: 'session_started', roomName }` to all connected WebSocket clients in that session
Inject ArenaGateway into ArenaController (it's already registered as a provider in ArenaModule)
Call `arenaGateway.broadcastSessionStarted(id, roomName)` from the start endpoint handler after successful room creation
In frontend `arena-session-context.tsx`, the `session_started` event is already handled by task 7's system case extension — no additional frontend work needed here
